title: use-after-free accessing RLS config after the LB policy is shut down
category: use-after-free (a picker reads policy state after the LB policy was destroyed)
crash_site: gRPC C-core ASAN heap-use-after-free reading an RLS load-balancing config during a pick, while the RLS LB policy is being shut down concurrently (channel teardown / resolver update).

ground_truth.decoy_fn: RlsLb::Cache::Entry::Pick / Cache::Entry::Size where freed state is also touched. The defect is the Picker holding/using config state whose owning RlsLb was shut down and destroyed.
ground_truth.fix_summary: RlsLb::Picker::Pick accessed config owned by the RlsLb policy without keeping it alive; after the policy was shut down and destroyed, an in-flight pick dereferenced the freed config -> UAF. Fix: have the Picker hold its own reference to the config so it outlives policy shutdown.

admission.note: Authored from PR #39316 (backport of #39303, fixes #39217) title+diff. crash != cause: UAF during a pick reading config; cause is RlsLb::Picker::Pick using config whose owning policy was shut down/destroyed (no ref held). Decoy = Cache::Entry::Pick. Pure C++ ownership across the policy/picker lifetime. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/grpc/grpc/pull/39316 — [RLS] fix use-after-free from accessing config after LB policy shutdown; fix 6c472088f7ce, base 26fe06e53783.
